edges.description: Impaired MMR leads to length alterations at microsatellite repeat sequences
Accumulated replication errors result in high somatic mutation burden
Microsatellite instability causes frameshift mutations in coding sequences
High mutation load generates abundant immunogenic neoantigens
Tumors adaptively upregulate checkpoint ligands to suppress T cell attack

nodes.description: Loss of MMR protein function (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, or PMS2) impairs the recognition and repair of DNA replication errors. The MMR system normally corrects base-base mismatches and insertion/deletion loops at microsatellite sequences. Deficiency leads to accumulation of thousands of somatic mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Spontaneous colonic transection following pathologic complete response to pembrolizumab in high microsatellite instability colorectal cancer: A case report and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: High microsatellite instability (MSI-H) colorectal cancer (CRC), caused by deficient mismatch repair, accounts for about 15% of all CRC cases and is more common in right-sided tumors.

nodes.description: Anti-PD-1 antibody with remarkable activity in MSI-H rectal cancer. A single-arm study demonstrated 100% clinical complete response rate in locally advanced MSI-H rectal cancer, allowing organ preservation without surgery or radiation.

evidence.reference_title: PD-1 Blockade in Mismatch Repair-Deficient, Locally Advanced Rectal Cancer.
evidence.snippet: All 12 patients (100%; 95% confidence interval, 74 to 100) had a clinical complete response, with no evidence of tumor on magnetic resonance imaging, 18F-fluorodeoxyglucose-positron-emission tomography, endoscopic evaluation, digital rectal examination, or biopsy.

nodes.description: Coding microsatellites in tumor suppressor genes (TGFbetaRII, BAX, ACVR2, IGF2R) accumulate frameshift mutations, inactivating their tumor suppressive functions. TGFbetaRII frameshift is particularly common, disrupting TGF-beta growth inhibition.

MSI-H tumors accumulate 10-100 times more somatic mutations than microsatellite stable tumors, resulting in tumor mutational burden often exceeding 10-20 mutations per megabase. This creates abundant neoantigens derived from mutant peptides presented on MHC molecules.

evidence.reference_title: Deficient mismatch repair/microsatellite unstable colorectal cancer: Diagnosis, prognosis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: The pathogenic alterations of MMR genes lead to the accumulation of frequent somatic mutational events

nodes.description: Microsatellites are repetitive DNA sequences highly susceptible to replication errors. Without functional MMR, these sequences accumulate insertions and deletions, detectable as length variations (instability) compared to normal tissue. MSI-H is defined as instability at two or more of five standard markers.

evidence.reference_title: Deficient mismatch repair/microsatellite unstable colorectal cancer: Diagnosis, prognosis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: Microsatellite unstable (MSI) colorectal cancers (CRCs) are due to DNA mismatch repair (MMR) deficiency

nodes.description: The high neoantigen burden in MSI-H tumors stimulates robust anti-tumor immunity. Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes, particularly CD8+ cytotoxic T cells, recognize and attack tumor cells presenting neoantigens. However, tumors upregulate PD-L1 as an adaptive resistance mechanism.

Combination checkpoint blockade with anti-PD-1 (nivolumab) and anti-CTLA-4 (ipilimumab) approved for MSI-H/dMMR metastatic CRC. CheckMate 142 showed high response rates and durable disease control with combined checkpoint inhibition.

evidence.reference_title: Durable Clinical Benefit With Nivolumab Plus Ipilimumab in DNA Mismatch Repair-Deficient/Microsatellite Instability-High Metastatic Colorectal Cancer.
evidence.snippet: investigator-assessed ORR was 55% (95% CI, 45.2 to 63.8), and disease control rate for ≥ 12 weeks was 80%
nivolumab plus ipilimumab significantly improved progression-free survival versus investigator’s choice of chemotherapy
nivolumab plus ipilimumab significantly improved progression-free survival versus nivolumab monotherapy across all treatment lines

nodes.description: MSI-H tumors upregulate PD-L1 expression in response to interferon-gamma from infiltrating T cells. PD-L1 engagement of PD-1 on T cells suppresses cytotoxic function, allowing immune evasion despite the immunogenic microenvironment. This makes MSI-H tumors vulnerable to PD-1/PD-L1 blockade.

biological_processes: Negative Regulation of T Cell Mediated Immunity

nodes.description: Anti-PD-1 immune checkpoint inhibitor approved as first-line monotherapy for metastatic MSI-H/dMMR colorectal cancer. KEYNOTE-177 demonstrated superior progression-free survival compared to chemotherapy with durable responses in a subset of patients.

evidence.reference_title: Pembrolizumab in Microsatellite-Instability-High Advanced Colorectal Cancer.
evidence.snippet: Pembrolizumab led to significantly longer progression-free survival than chemotherapy when received as first-line therapy for MSI-H-dMMR metastatic colorectal cancer
